title: TotalEnergies CEO Expects EU Sustainable Aviation Fuel Mandate to be Dropped In Future
content: TotalEnergies CEO Patrick Pouyanne said in Davos that the EU would likely drop its mandate to incorporate sustainable aviation fuel in future, comparing it to the blocâ€™s plan to drop its ban on new combustionâ€‘engine cars from 2035. He stated that TotalEnergies could produce sustainable aviation fuel, but airlines were unwilling to pay four times the amount paid for regular jet fuel. Pouyanne noted that airlines were resisting the requirement and argued that pricing concerns remained the biggest barrier for adoption. He added that despite accusations from airlines claiming insufficient production, the company had the capability to supply sustainable aviation fuel but demand was limited to what was needed for regulatory compliance. He emphasised that the situation mirrored previous EU regulatory reversals and highlighted the reluctance of airlines to pay higher costs for sustainable fuel
author: Analysts Account
